source_title: DexUMI: Using Human Hand as the Universal Manipulation Interface for Dexterous Manipulation

ai_summary.one_liner: DexUMI bridges kinematic and visual gaps between human and robot hands via exoskeleton hardware and AI inpainting to enable efficient dexterous manipulation skill transfer.
ai_summary.problem: Teaching dexterous manipulation to robot hands is blocked by two gaps: kinematic gap (human and robot hand morphologies differ) and visual gap (demonstration data shows human hands, causing distribution shift during robot execution).
ai_summary.solution: Hardware adaptation via wearable exoskeleton with bi-level workspace-matching optimization; software adaptation via SAM2 segmentation + AI inpainting to replace human hand appearances with robot hand images in collected demonstrations.
ai_summary.key_result: Average 86% task success rate across 4 challenging real-world tasks on two different robot platforms; 3.2× higher data collection efficiency than teleoperation.
limitations: Requires per-robot-hand exoskeleton design and fabrication
Camera must be rigidly attached to robot hand/exoskeleton
Robot hand images for inpainting still require real hardware in the loop

research_gaps.question: 실험 태스크가 단일 고정 시점 카메라 환경에서 진행되어, 다양한 카메라 배치나 비정형 환경에서의 일반화 가능성은 미검증
